---
title: "My Awesome Paper Title"
author: "Your Name"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
---

## Abstract
This is a single-file Quarto website hosted on GitHub Pages. It combines Markdown text, LaTeX math, and interactive 3D elements.

## Methodology
You can include complex math like $$E = mc^2$$ and immediately follow it with an interactive 3D figure to visualize your results.

```{=html}
<script src="[https://cdn.babylonjs.com/babylon.js](https://cdn.babylonjs.com/babylon.js)"></script>

<canvas id="renderCanvas" style="width: 100%; height: 400px; touch-action: none; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #eee;"></canvas>

<script>
    const canvas = document.getElementById("renderCanvas");
    const engine = new BABYLON.Engine(canvas, true);

    const createScene = function () {
        const scene = new BABYLON.Scene(engine);
        scene.clearColor = new BABYLON.Color4(0.95, 0.95, 0.95, 1); // Light grey background

        // Camera: Allows user to rotate around the cube
        const camera = new BABYLON.ArcRotateCamera("camera", -Math.PI / 2, Math.PI / 2.5, 3, new BABYLON.Vector3(0, 0, 0), scene);
        camera.attachControl(canvas, true);

        // Light: Hemispheric light provides soft ambient lighting
        const light = new BABYLON.HemisphericLight("light", new BABYLON.Vector3(0, 1, 0), scene);
        light.intensity = 0.7;

        // The Cube
        const box = BABYLON.MeshBuilder.CreateBox("box", {size: 1}, scene);
        
        // Add some color to the cube
        const material = new BABYLON.StandardMaterial("mat", scene);
        material.diffuseColor = new BABYLON.Color3(0.2, 0.5, 0.8); // Nice blue
        box.material = material;

        // Animation: Spin the cube
        scene.registerBeforeRender(function () {
            box.rotation.y += 0.01;
            box.rotation.x += 0.005;
        });

        return scene;
    };

    const scene = createScene();

    // Start the engine
    engine.runRenderLoop(function () {
        scene.render();
    });

    // Handle window resizing
    window.addEventListener("resize", function () {
        engine.resize();
    });
</script>
